# Office AI Agents — Current Technical Specification
*Auto-generated by audit on 2026-06-20. Source of truth.*

This document describes how the system actually behaves right now, verified
against `config/*.json`, `workers/*.js`, `wrangler.toml`, the GitHub Actions
workflow, and live D1 query results. Where the code disagrees with
`agents-config.json`'s narrative descriptions or the original design intent,
the code wins and the discrepancy is called out below.

---

## Simulation Schedule

- **Worker cron (`wrangler.toml`):** `*/30 5-13 * * *` — every 30 minutes,
  05:00-13:30 UTC = **08:00-16:30 Israel Daylight Time (IDT, UTC+3)**, every
  day of the week (cron has no day-of-week restriction). Each tick,
  `agent-runner.js`'s `scheduled()` converts to Israel local time
  (`israelTimeParts()`, hardcoded `ISRAEL_UTC_OFFSET_HOURS = 3` — must be
  manually updated when Israel switches to/from IST) and calls
  `runScheduledBlock(env, time, dayOfWeek)`.
- **`runScheduledBlock()` is a no-op** unless `config/daily-schedule.json`
  has a block at that exact `time` for that `dayOfWeek`. Most of the 17
  ticks/day are cheap no-ops.
- **GitHub Actions cron (`.github/workflows/scheduled-claude.yml`):**
  `30 23 * * 0-4` — 23:30 UTC = **02:30 IL, Sunday-Thursday only**. Runs a
  direct Anthropic-API session (`run-claude-session.js`) that executes one
  full simulated work day end-to-end and commits a report. Friday/Saturday
  nights are intentionally skipped (Israeli weekend).
- **Time compression:** `config/simulation-config.json`'s `TIME_SCALE`
  block states "24 real hours = 1 simulated work week of 5 days," but
  **this is not actually how the day advances**. In the live code,
  `runWorkDayCycle()`/`runScheduledBlock()` advance `current_day` by
  exactly 1 every time they run to completion — so the simulated-time rate
  is determined by *how often a full day cycle completes* (once per
  GitHub Actions night run, or once per Worker-cron day if a human/Claude
  session triggers `POST /api/agents/trigger {"type":"day"}"`), not by a
  24-real-hour/5-sim-day formula enforced anywhere in code.
- **Active vs. skipped days** (`config/daily-schedule.json` `week_mapping`,
  `dayOfWeek = ((current_day - 1) % 7) + 1`):
  - Sun-Thu (1-5): full work day — 5 case batches, tool-task window
    (Sun-Thu only), AI-experience report block, daily standup, spare time.
  - Friday (6): short day — 2 case batches (60%/40% share), AI-experience
    report, weekly executive summary + standup, spare time.
  - Saturday (7): full day off — zero case batches, zero meetings, zero
    Gemini/Claude/Groq calls (`force_idle: true`), pure idle.

## AI Models in Use

| Model | Provider | Role | Tier | Daily limit |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `groq/llama3-8b-8192` | Groq | **Primary** model for routine per-case work, all agents (`agent-base.js queryGemini()` → `groq-client.js callGroq()`) | Free | ~14,400 req/day, resets 00:00 UTC |
| `@cf/meta/llama-3.1-8b-instruct-fp8` | Cloudflare Workers AI | Case routing/classification (`callCFRouter()`, runs before every case is dispatched) AND same-session fallback when Groq is down or Gemini 429s (`callCloudflareFallback()`) | Free, account-scoped `AI` binding, no extra credentials | ~10,000 req/day, resets 00:00 UTC |
| `gemini-2.5-flash-lite` | Google AI Studio | Report synthesis ONLY — monthly/quarterly/semi-yearly/yearly meetings (`meeting-engine.js`) | Paid free-tier | ~1,500 req/day, resets 11:00 IL |
| `claude-sonnet-4-6` | Anthropic | App's AI Search bar (`data-center-api`) + hard-case escalations + Agent 10's root-level GitHub Issue review | Paid | **Hard cap: 5 calls/day across ALL agents combined** |
| `human+claude-code` | n/a | Agent 10 (Architect)'s "model" for routine cases — never calls an AI; cases are logged for mood bookkeeping only and filed as a single GitHub Issue (`claude-action`, `architect-task`) for human/Claude-Code review | n/a | n/a |

Confirmed by live D1 (`interactions.model_source`, all-time): **groq 89,
claude 15, null 39** (null = idle/coworker-chat/non-API interaction rows, or
rows logged before `model_source` tracking was added). No `gemini` or
`cloudflare-fallback` rows have ever been recorded — Gemini has not yet been
asked to synthesize a single report, and the Cloudflare fallback has never
been triggered.

## Active Agents

Per `config/agents-config.json` `_meta` and `AGENT_CLASSES` in
`agent-runner.js`:

| ID | Name | Status | Class | Model (per-case) | Clearance | Case load (quota) | Live sessions (all-time) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | The Perfectionist | active | `PerfectionistAgent` (dedicated) | Groq (primary) | standard | 30-50/day | 35 |
| 2 | The Productive | active | `ProductiveAgent` (dedicated) | Groq (primary) | standard | 30-50/day | 30 |
| 3 | The Standard Agent | active | `StandardAgent` (dedicated) | Groq (primary) | standard | 30-50/day | 30 |
| 4 | The Trainee | active | `TraineeAgent` (dedicated) | Groq (primary) | standard | 30-50/day | 24 |
| 5 | The IT Chief | specified (runs via stub) | `StubAgent` | Groq (primary) | sudo | 15-30/day | 44 |
| 6 | The QA | specified (runs via stub) | `StubAgent` | Groq (primary) | sudo | n/a (audit cases only) | **2** (stale — last active 2026-06-11) |
| 7 | The Team Lead | specified (runs via stub) | `StubAgent` | Groq (primary) | sudo | n/a (management cases only) | **0 — never run** |
| 8 | The Lead QA | specified (runs via stub) | `StubAgent` | Groq (primary) | sudo | n/a (audit cases only) | **0 — never run** |
| 9 | The Designer | specified (runs via stub) | `StubAgent` | Groq (primary) | specialist | n/a (design/UX cases) | 1 |
| 10 | The Architect | specified (runs via stub) | `StubAgent`, special-cased | **none** (`human+claude-code`) | root | n/a (root escalations) | 3 |
| 11 | The CEO | specified (runs via stub) | `StubAgent` | Groq (primary) | root | n/a (unique-client/meeting cases) | 14 |

All 11 agents are wired into the simulation and instantiable; "active" vs.
"specified" in the config only distinguishes whether an agent has a
dedicated `agent-N-*.js` class (1-4) or runs generically through
`agent-stub.js` reading its full behavioral spec from `agents-config.json`
(5-11) — both groups are live and case-eligible. Agent 10 is hard-coded
(`processArchitectCaseBatch()`) to never call any AI model for its cases.

## Daily Cycle — What Actually Happens

Per **per-block Cron Trigger tick** (the live, wired path — `runScheduledBlock()`):

1. Cron fires every 30 min, 08:00-16:30 IDT. Converted to Israel time;
   no-op unless `daily-schedule.json` has a block at this exact time.
2. **First due block of the day**: `getYearState()` reads the latest D1
   `year_stats` row, increments `current_day`, computes `dayOfWeek`, picks
   the day-type schedule (full/Friday/Saturday), generates the day's ~50
   cases (`generateAssignedDailyBatch()` in `crm-engine.js`, doubled if
   `inspection_mode`), persists them to D1, and partitions them across the
   day's `case_batch` blocks by `case_share`. This "cycle" is cached in
   `SIM_KV` (`daily-cycle-state`) between ticks.
3. **Each `case_batch` block** (e.g. 08:00 30%, 09:30 20%, 11:00 20%, 13:00
   20%, 14:30 10%): every case in the batch is routed/classified for free
   via Cloudflare Workers AI (`callCFRouter()`), grouped by assigned agent,
   and run through that agent's `handleCase()`. Agent 10's cases instead go
   through `processArchitectCaseBatch()` (no AI call, logged + filed as one
   GitHub Issue). ~30% of low-quality (<0.5) interactions trigger a
   simulated "compare alternatives" check. Any interaction below quality
   0.6 is queued for the model-education program.
4. **11:30 `tool_task_window`** (Sun-Thu only): checks `ai-tools.json`'s
   `weekly_rotation` for today; if the assigned standing-project board item
   is `queued` and not yet filed, opens one GitHub Issue (`asset-task`) —
   no programmatic creative-tool calls are ever made (human-in-the-loop).
5. **15:30 `report` block**: every agent who handled ≥1 case today files a
   1-2 sentence in-character AI-experience status note (1 Groq/Gemini call
   each via `agent.queryGemini()`), and up to 3 of the day's worst-quality
   interactions become structured "model education case studies" (D1
   `reports` row + GitHub Issue if `GITHUB_TOKEN` is set).
6. **16:00 `meeting` (daily_standup)**: runs `meeting-engine.js`'s
   `daily_standup` meeting type.
7. **16:00 `spare_time`**: every instantiated agent with no more cases gets
   either a 20%-chance 1-Gemini/Groq-call coworker chat, or (80% chance,
   100% on Saturday) goes fully idle with zero API calls — the primary
   token-discipline lever.
8. **Friday-only `weekly_summary` block (12:00)**: generates and commits
   3 files under `reports/weekly/` (markdown executive summary, CSV data
   export, public excerpt), bumps any `implemented` asset-pipeline product
   versions by +0.01, and runs the `weekly` meeting type.
9. **Last due block of the day** triggers `finalizeScheduledDay()`: builds
   the final per-agent summary, may start/advance narrative side plots
   (`side-plots.json` triggers — rivalry escalation, client crisis,
   breakthrough, comparison event, inspiration event, meeting tension),
   checks year-tracker milestones (day 30/90/180/270/365 → monthly/
   quarterly/semi-yearly/quarterly/yearly meetings), persists the new
   `year_stats` row, commits `reports/daily/day-NNN-summary.md` to GitHub,
   and clears the `SIM_KV` cycle state.
10. Per-block errors (e.g. a Gemini 429) are caught and logged as a D1
    `reports` incident row (filed under Agent 10) — a single bad tick never
    aborts the day or triggers Cloudflare cron retries.

**Separately**, the nightly GitHub Actions job (`scheduled-claude.yml`,
23:30 UTC Sun-Thu) runs a parallel/alternate path: a direct Claude session
(`run-claude-session.js`) that's instructed to simulate one full day in a
single shot rather than relying on the per-block cron ticks.

## Token Economy — Actual Rules

Per `config/token-economy.json` and the code that enforces it
(`agent-base.js`'s Claude-cap check is referenced from `PENDING-WORK.md`'s
2026-06-16 session, not re-verified line-by-line in this audit's read
scope, but the live D1 numbers — 15 Claude interactions total, never more
than 5/day per the cap design — are consistent with it being enforced):

- **Claude (`claude-sonnet-4-6`): hard cap of 5 calls/day across ALL agents
  combined.** Once hit, `interactWithApp()` falls back to Groq (free) —
  the simulation never halts on the Claude cap.
- **Groq is primary** for all routine per-case work, run hard until its
  free-tier quota (~14,400/day) is exhausted.
- **Cloudflare Workers AI is the fallback** when Groq is unavailable or
  Gemini 429s, and is *also* used unconditionally for cheap case
  routing/classification before every case is dispatched.
- **Gemini is reserved exclusively for report synthesis** (monthly,
  quarterly, semi-yearly, yearly meetings) per
  `report_models_by_meeting_type` — daily standups, weekly meetings,
  emergency huddles, audits, coaching, and PIP sessions all route through
  Groq instead, contrary to the original "one Gemini engine" design.
- **Agent 10 (Architect) never consumes any model quota** for routine
  cases — `architect_model: "human+claude-code"`.
- **Spare time is the main token-saving lever**: 80% idle (100% on
  Saturday), only 20% chance of a single coworker-chat call.

## GitHub Actions Automation

- **File:** `.github/workflows/scheduled-claude.yml`.
- **What it does:** on `30 23 * * 0-4` (02:30 IL, Sun-Thu) or manual
  `workflow_dispatch`, checks out the repo, runs
  `node .github/scripts/run-claude-session.js` (a direct Anthropic-API
  session that reads `CLAUDE.md`/`TOKEN-BUDGET.md` and executes one
  simulated office day), then `bash .github/scripts/commit-and-log.sh` to
  commit results and append a one-line log entry to `TOKEN-BUDGET.md`.
- **Required secrets:** `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` (required), `GROQ_API_KEY`
  (required), `GOOGLE_AI_API_KEY` (optional). Could not be verified
  programmatically this session (no `gh` CLI available) — confirm manually
  at `github.com/avivnofar/office-AI-agents/settings/secrets/actions`.
- **Current status:** code-correct and consistent (paths point at the repo
  root post-migration, cron is the single surviving job per the 2026-06-16
  cleanup). Whether it is *actually succeeding* on a given Sun-Thu night
  could not be confirmed this session without GitHub Actions run-history
  access — D1's last `agent_sessions` activity is 2026-06-19, one day
  before this audit.

## Cloudflare Worker

- **Name:** `data-center-agents` (`workers/agent-runner.js` is `main`).
- **What it does:** runs the per-block simulation cron, exposes
  `/api/agents/*` admin endpoints (status, sessions, reports, suggestions,
  year state, side plots, manual trigger, Gemini smoke test) gated by
  `X-Admin-Token`, and `/api/simulation` for pause/inspection-mode control.
- **Bindings:**
  - `DB` — D1 `data-center-db` (schema: agents, agent_sessions, cases,
    interactions, reports, suggestions, weekly_analytics, meetings,
    side_plots, promotions, year_stats).
  - `AGENT_STATE` — Durable Object (`AgentStateDO`, per-agent persisted
    mood/irritation/configOverrides).
  - `SIM_KV` — KV namespace for live simulation overrides
    (`simulation-state`) and the in-progress day cycle
    (`daily-cycle-state`).
  - `APP_API` — service binding to `data-center-api` (works around
    Cloudflare error 1042: a Worker can't `fetch()` another Worker's
    `*.workers.dev` URL directly).
  - `AI` — Cloudflare Workers AI, account-scoped, no extra credentials
    (case routing + 429/outage fallback).
- **Cron trigger:** `*/30 5-13 * * *` (every 30 min, 08:00-16:30 IDT, all
  7 days — the day-of-week gating happens inside `daily-schedule.json`,
  not the cron expression itself).

## Known Issues or TODOs

- **No `TODO`/`FIXME` comments found** in `workers/*.js` — the codebase is
  clean of inline markers; outstanding work lives in `PENDING-WORK.md`
  and `TOKEN-BUDGET.md`'s `⏳ Next` section instead.
- **`PENDING-WORK.md`'s logged sessions are entirely about the
  `data-center` app** (1COM/MirtaPBX knowledge-base entries, RTL audits,
  AI Search UI), not the office simulation engine itself — this file's
  history predates/spans the repo split and hasn't been refreshed with
  office-specific pending items since the migration.
- **Agents 7 and 8 (Team Lead, Lead QA) have never run a single session**
  — 0 rows in D1 `agent_sessions` despite both being wired into
  `AGENT_CLASSES`/`StubAgent` and case-eligible per `crm-engine.js`'s 20%
  admin-share fix (logged in `TOKEN-BUDGET.md` 2026-06-18 as "Agents 6-9
  scheduling fixed" — evidently 7 and 8 are still not receiving cases or
  report/spare-time instantiation despite that fix).
- **Agent 6 (QA) is stale** — only 2 sessions total, last active
  2026-06-11, predating most subsequent fixes.
- **Agent 9 (Designer) has only 1 session ever** despite being scheduled
  for Tuesday/Thursday tool-task windows and Friday-style admin
  case-share — likely under-exercised by the actual daily run cadence.
- **Gemini has never been called** (0 `gemini` rows in `interactions`,
  all-time) — no monthly/quarterly/semi-yearly/yearly meeting has fired
  yet, consistent with the simulation being well under day 30 (the first
  milestone).
- **Cloudflare Workers AI fallback has never fired** (0
  `cloudflare-fallback` rows) — Groq has not yet been observed to fail.
- **GitHub Actions secrets** (`ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `GROQ_API_KEY`) could
  not be verified present this session — flagged for manual confirmation.
- **DST drift risk**: `ISRAEL_UTC_OFFSET_HOURS = 3` in `agent-runner.js`
  and the cron window in `wrangler.toml`/`scheduled-claude.yml` are all
  hardcoded for IDT (UTC+3, roughly Mar-Oct) and require a manual update
  when Israel switches to IST (UTC+2).

## Differences From Original Design

- **Time compression is not a real "24h = 1 week" engine.** The original
  `TIME_SCALE` spec (24 real hours = 1 simulated 5-day week) is not
  enforced by any code path; simulated days advance once per completed
  day-cycle (nightly GitHub Action or a full per-block cron day), not on
  a fixed real-time ratio.
- **Gemini's role shrank from "the one engine" to "reports only."**
  `simulation-config.json`'s `GEMINI` block and `CLAUDE.md`'s historical
  "one Gemini engine, not eleven Workers" strategy describe Gemini as the
  single role-playing engine for all 11 agents. In the live
  `token-economy.json`/code, Gemini is now reserved exclusively for
  monthly+ report synthesis; **Groq is the actual primary model for all
  routine per-case agent work**, with Cloudflare Workers AI as the
  free fallback/router. This was a deliberate fix (documented in
  `TOKEN-BUDGET.md` 2026-06-16) for recurring Gemini 429 quota errors.
- **Model distribution is 5-way, not 1-way.** Routine cases (Groq) +
  routing/fallback (Cloudflare) + reports (Gemini) + AI Search/escalations
  (Claude, capped at 5/day) + Architect (no model at all) — a much more
  distributed architecture than the original single-engine design.
- **"Active" agents is really all 11, not just 1-4.** `simulation-config.json`'s
  `SIMULATION.active_agents: [1,2,3,4]` / `stub_agents: [5..11]` framing
  undersells it: agents 5-11 are fully wired, case-eligible, and have run
  live sessions (except 7 and 8, which is a bug, not a design choice) —
  they just share the generic `StubAgent` class instead of having
  dedicated `agent-N-*.js` files like 1-4.
- **Per-block cron, not one mega-invocation.** `daily-schedule.json`'s
  `_meta.cron_status` notes the day was originally meant to run as a
  single `runWorkDayCycle()` call; the live system instead spreads case
  batches, the tool-task window, reports, standup, spare time, and the
  Friday weekly summary across the day via 9 separate per-block Cron
  Trigger ticks (`runScheduledBlock()`), with state persisted in `SIM_KV`
  between ticks.
- **A second, independent automation path exists** (the nightly GitHub
  Actions job) that the original Worker-only cron design didn't account
  for — it runs a full simulated day via a direct Claude session rather
  than the per-block Cloudflare cron, creating two parallel ways a
  simulated day can advance.
- **Case-volume design note** (`daily-schedule.json` itself flags this):
  per-agent `quotas` in `agents-config.json` (e.g. 30-50/agent) look like
  a mandate to generate 30-50 cases × 11 agents/day, but the actual CRM
  pool stays at ~50 cases/day total, spread across blocks — quotas are
  capacity ceilings, not generation targets.
